name: Antigua and Barbuda
summary: Antigua and Barbuda is a Caribbean common-law constitutional monarchy whose family-law framework operates under the Divorce Act, the Marriage Act, the Maintenance of Children Act, the Status of Children Act, and case-law applying the welfare-of-the-child principle. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by case-law and Maintenance of Children Act provisions. The Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court (Court of Appeal sitting for Antigua and Barbuda) is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction was retained with the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the High Court (Family Division) and Magistrates' Courts. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health, Wellness and the Environment framework. Antigua and Barbuda is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the welfare-of-the-child principle. Antigua and Barbuda is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Maintenance of Children Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute on maintenance obligations including child support.

statutory_framework.title: Status of Children Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute on legal status of children.

apex_courts.name: Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court — Court of Appeal
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for OECS states including Antigua and Barbuda.

apex_courts.name: Judicial Committee of the Privy Council
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Antigua and Barbuda.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, Wellness and the Environment, Antigua and Barbuda
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Antiguan family-court decisions are anonymised per Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Maintenance of Children Act 1949 + colonial-era statutory framework
key_developments.description: Maintenance of Children Act 1949 enacted within colonial-era framework drawing on English-statutory-heritage. Foundational statutory framework persisting through independence transition for child-maintenance obligations and protection.

key_developments.title: Associated State of Antigua + internal-self-government framework
key_developments.description: Antigua became Associated State of West Indies 27 February 1967 — establishing internal-self-government framework with United Kingdom retaining defence and foreign affairs. Foundational pre-independence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic trajectory.

key_developments.title: Antigua and Barbuda independence + Vere Bird presidency-of-government
key_developments.description: Antigua and Barbuda achieved independence from the United Kingdom 1 November 1981 — establishing constitutional monarchy framework retaining JCPC as final appellate court. Vere Bird (Sr.) Antigua Labour Party first prime minister 1981-1994 — substantively distinctive founding-prime-minister framework affecting subsequent political-institutional trajectory.

key_developments.title: Antigua and Barbuda ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Antigua and Barbuda ratified the UNCRC on 5 October 1993 — framing the family-law-reform trajectory toward best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within OECS-Anglophone common-law framework.

key_developments.title: Lester Bird premiership + Antigua Labour Party dynastic-continuation
key_developments.description: Lester Bird (son of Vere Bird Sr.) Antigua Labour Party premiership 1994-2004 — substantively distinctive Caribbean dynastic-political-leadership pattern. Subsequent ALP defeat 2004 ending 28-year-Bird-family-dynasty framework.

key_developments.title: Divorce Act CAP 150 + statutory consolidation
key_developments.description: Divorce Act CAP 150 enacted 1997 — federal divorce statute drawing on English-divorce-statute substantive heritage. Foundational statutory framework for divorce-related family-law-jurisdiction.

key_developments.title: Baldwin Spencer + United Progressive Party + dynastic-defeat framework
key_developments.description: Baldwin Spencer (United Progressive Party) elected Prime Minister 24 March 2004 — substantively significant Bird-dynasty-defeat ending 28-year ALP rule. Subsequent UPP government 2004-2014 affecting family-law-modernisation trajectory.

key_developments.title: Status of Children Act 2008 + child-protection framework consolidation
key_developments.description: Status of Children Act 2008 enacted — federal statute on legal status of children including legitimacy, paternity, and inheritance reform. Substantively significant child-protection-framework consolidation within Caribbean OECS framework.

key_developments.title: Gaston Browne ALP-return + ABLP era + continuing dynastic-leadership
key_developments.description: Gaston Browne (Antigua Barbuda Labour Party) elected Prime Minister 12 June 2014 — substantively significant ALP-return-to-power framework. Subsequent re-elections 2018, 2023 affecting family-law-modernisation including 2018 referendum on Caribbean Court of Justice replacing JCPC (rejected).

key_developments.title: Browne re-election + Child Welfare Authority + Barbuda local-government framework
key_developments.description: Gaston Browne (ABLP) re-elected 18 January 2023 substantively significant continuing-premiership framework. Child Welfare Authority institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Status of Children Act 2008 + Divorce Act CAP 150 framework. Barbuda local-government framework substantively significant federal-island institutional context. Substantively significant Eastern Caribbean institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: ECSC + JCPC — welfare-of-the-child substantive register + republic referendum-discussion
key_developments.description: Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court and Judicial Committee of the Privy Council continue to develop welfare-of-the-child jurisprudence under Divorce Act CAP 150 + Maintenance of Children Act 1949 + Status of Children Act 2008 framework in custody disputes without adopting the 'parental alienation' label as a doctrinal term. Substantive republic-referendum discussions continuing through 2020s-2030s within Caribbean Realm-decolonisation framework.

structural_findings: Antigua and Barbuda operates a common-law framework — places Antigua and Barbuda in the OECS-Anglophone Caribbean common-law cluster.
Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court appellate jurisdiction places Antigua and Barbuda in the OECS-shared-judicial-system cluster.
Judicial Committee of the Privy Council retention as final appellate court is shared with other OECS Anglophone states.
Non-Hague Convention status places Antigua and Barbuda in the non-Hague Caribbean cluster.

sources.title: Judicial Committee of the Privy Council

editorial_notes: Antigua and Barbuda jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 2 to 10 key_developments with full Vere-Bird-to-Gaston-Browne trajectory: 1949-Maintenance-of-Children-Act-+-colonial-era + 1967-Associated-State-of-Antigua + 1981-independence-+-Vere-Bird-presidency-of-government + 1991-1993-UNCRC-ratification + 1994-Lester-Bird-premiership-+-Antigua-Labour-Party-dynastic-continuation + 1997-Divorce-Act-CAP-150 + 2004-Baldwin-Spencer-+-United-Progressive-Party-+-dynastic-defeat + 2008-Status-of-Children-Act + 2014-Gaston-Browne-ALP-return-+-ABLP-era + 2024-ECSC-+-JCPC-welfare-of-the-child-+-republic-referendum-discussion.
Common-law Caribbean (OECS-ECSC appellate + JCPC final-appellate + non-Hague Convention).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive welfare-of-the-child analysis under Divorce Act CAP 150 + Maintenance of Children Act 1949 + Status of Children Act 2008 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins OECS-Anglophone-Caribbean + common-law + OECS-shared-judicial-system + JCPC-final-appellate + Bird-family-dynasty-1981-2004-distinctive + CCJ-referendum-2018-rejected + Browne-ABLP-return-2014 + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
